            Correct Answer: Pakistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The act applies to all provinces and regions of Pakistan, regulating Hajj and Umrah services nationwide.

            204

            Muavineen are responsible for

            Correct Answer: Guiding Pilgrims
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Guiding Pilgrims
            Muavineen (assistants) help guide pilgrims during Hajj and Umrah, ensuring they follow the correct routes and rituals.

            205

            Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has ______ official languages.

            Correct Answer: 3
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • OIC has Arabic, English, and French as its three official languages.

            Additional information:

            • The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), founded on September 25, 1969.
            • It initially had 25 founding members.
            • It is headquartered in Jeddah.
            • Formerly known as the Organization of the Islamic Conference, it changed its name on June 28, 2011.
            • With 57 current member countries.
            • Jakarta is the largest city among OIC member nations.

            Last verified on 08-06-2026

            206

            Why are environmental and safety protocols likely to be included in the draft agreements for Hajj arrangements in 2025?

            Correct Answer: To guarantee the health, safety, and well-being of pilgrims during their participation in Hajj rituals
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            To guarantee the health, safety, and well-being of pilgrims during their participation in Hajj rituals are environmental and safety protocols likely to be included in the draft agreements for Hajj arrangements in 2025

            207

            What is one of the benefits of using the Rehnuma-e-Hajj booklet during Hajj 2025?

            Correct Answer: It helps pilgrims avoid common mistakes related to religious practices specific to their sect
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The benefit of using the Rehnuma-e-Hajj booklet during Hajj 2025 is It helps pilgrims avoid common mistakes related to religious practices specific to their sect

            208

            Who are the primary parties involved in drafting agreements for Hajj arrangements in 2025?

            Correct Answer: Hajj mission authorities local service providers and the Saudi government
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hajj mission authorities, local service providers, and the Saudi government are the primary parties involved in drafting agreements for Hajj arrangements in 2025

            209

            What is one of the key features depicted on the Mina and Arafat map for Hajj 2025 to help guide pilgrims?

            Correct Answer: The locations of medical centers and emergency facilities
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             The locations of medical centers and emergency facilities is one of the key features depicted on the Mina and Arafat map for Hajj 2025 to help guide pilgrims

            210

            Why is the Mina and Arafat map crucial for ensuring pilgrim safety during Hajj 2025?

            Correct Answer: It allows pilgrims to avoid overcrowded areas and locate safe paths
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             The Mina and Arafat map is crucial for ensuring pilgrim safety during Hajj 2025 It allows pilgrims to avoid overcrowded areas and locate safe paths

            211

            What ritual is performed by pilgrims during Ayam-e-Tashreeq?

            Correct Answer: Stoning the Jamarat
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Ayam-e-Tashreeq, performs the ritual of "stoning the devil

            From the 11th-13th of Dhul Hijjah, known as Ayyam al-Tashreeq,

            Pilgrims spend time in Mina where they throw pebbles at all three Jamarat


            212

            When does a pilgrim perform Umrah in Hajj Tamattu'?

            Correct Answer: Before the 8th of Dhu al-Hijjah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hajj Tamattu enters Ihram on the eighth day of Dhul-Hijjah

            The pilgrim who performs Hajj-ul-Tamattu is called a Mutamatti. Hajj-ul-Tamattuj

            It means to perform Umrah during the season of Hajj.

            In Hajj-ul-Tamattuj a pilgrim assumes Ihram for only Umrah.

            After reaching Makkah, he performs Tawaf and Saa’ee for Umrah.

            He then performs Qasr (shaves or clips his hair). Here, the pilgrim has to sacrifice an animal.

            213

            What is Rukn Yamani in the context of the Kaaba?

            Correct Answer: The corner located on the southern side of the Kaaba
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Rukn al-Yamani is the name of the southwest corner of the Kaaba, which is a cube-shaped 

            The Rukn al-Yamani is one of the four pillars of the Kaaba

            The small piece of land between Rukn-e-Islam (the Black Stone) and Rukn-e-Yamani (the Yemeni Corner) 

            It is known as the "Hatim" or "Hijr Ismail."

            214

            The Mataaf is located inside which of the following?

            Correct Answer: Masjid al-Haram
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Mataf is the open, flat area surrounding the Kaaba within Masjid al-Haram,

            Where pilgrims perform the Tawaf.

            North-East is Marwah located from the Kaaba

            215

            According to Saudi government rules, each pilgrim is allowed to take only ____ liters of Zamzam water to their countries.

            Correct Answer: 5
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pilgrims receive Zamzam water at King Abdulaziz International Airport in Jeddah

             Pilgrims can carry up to 5 liters of Zamzam water ·

            Maximum 5 kg/litres of Zamzam is allowed ·

            216

            Certificates not accepted by KSA?

            Correct Answer: Certificates issued by private clinics
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Certificates issued by private clinics are not recognized by the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia for official purposes.

            217

            The name of second Kalimah is _____?

            Correct Answer: Kalimah Sahadat
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            List of six Kalimas

            1

            كَلِمَاتْ اَلطَّيِّبَة‎

            Kalimat aṭ-Ṭayyibah

            (Word of Purity)

            2

            كَلِمَاتْ اَلشَّهَادَة‎

            Kalimat ash-Shahādah

            (Word of Testimony)

            3

            كَلِمَاتْ اَلتَّمْجِيدْ‎

            Kalimat at-Tamjīd

            (Word of Majesty)

            4

            كَلِمَاتْ اَلتَّوْحِيدْ‎

            Kalimat at-Tawḥīd

            (Word of Oneness)

            5

            كَلِمَاتْ إِسْتِغْفَارْ‎

            Kalimat ʾIstighfār

            (Word of Penitence)

            6

            كَلِمَاتْ رَدّْ اَلْكُفْرْ‎

            Kalimat Radd al-Kufr

            ("Word of Rejection of Disbelief")


            218

            On which of the following occasion, Muslim offers 'Namaz-e-Kusuf?

            Correct Answer: At the time of eclipse of sun
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Salat ul-Kusuf is a prayer of two rakaaht performed during a solar eclipse.

            Namaz-e-Khasoof (نماز خسوف) is offered during a lunar eclipse.

            Namaz-e-Kasoof (نماز کسوف) is offered during a solar eclipse.


            219

            What is the title given to Hazrat Usman (R.A)?

            Correct Answer: Zun-Noorain
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Usman (R.A)

            Belonged

            Quraysh

            Born

            Makkah

            Caliph

            Third caliph

            Caliphate

            23 to 35 Hijjah

            Caliphate

            644 to 656 CE

            Titles

            Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

            Martyred

            18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

            Martyrdom day

            Friday

            Wives

            Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

            Full Name

            Usman bin Affaan

            Death cause

            Martyred by rebels

            Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

            Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

            Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

             Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

            Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 

            220

            What is the Islamic law regrading the protection of human life?

            Correct Answer: All of these
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Qisas refers to retributive justice in Islamic law, ensuring fair punishment for crimes like murder.
          • Sanctity of human life and right to self-defense are fundamental principles in Islam, emphasizing protection and justice.
            • 221

            What is the first obligation of Islam?

            Correct Answer: Declaration of faith (Shahada)
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Shahada is the first pillar of Islam.
          • It affirming belief in the oneness of Allah and the prophethood of Muhammad (PBUH).
          • It is the fundamental declaration that makes one a Muslim: "There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad (PBUH) is His Messenger."
            • 222

            Which surah contains the longest verse in the Holy Quran, detailing the laws and principles of financial transactions?

            Correct Answer: Surah Al-Baqarah
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • The longest verse in the Quran is Ayat al-Mudayanah (Verse 282) of Surah Al-Baqarah.
          • It details financial transactions, including the importance of written agreements and witnesses in debt contracts.
          • It emphasizes honesty, clarity, and record-keeping in financial dealings to prevent disputes.
            • 223

            In which battle did the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) strictly enforce the rule that non-combatants, such as women and children, should not be harmed, setting an example of warfare in Islam?

            Correct Answer: Battle of Badr
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • In the Battle of Badr (624 CE), Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) emphasized ethical warfare, strictly prohibiting harm to non-combatants, including women, children, and the elderly.
          • This battle set a precedent in Islamic military ethics, promoting justice and humane treatment even during conflict.
            • 224

            In which Surah of the Holy Quran, rights of the women have been explained?

            Correct Answer: Surah Al-Talaq
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Surah Al-Talaq (The Divorce) discusses women's rights in matters of divorce, maintenance, and waiting period (Iddah).
          • It provides guidance on fair treatment and financial responsibilities towards women after divorce.
            • 225

            The Battle of Safeen (Saffin) was fought in 37 A.H. between Hazrat Ali (RA) and _______?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Muawia (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Battle of Siffin took place in 657 CE (37 AH).

            It was fought between Caliph Ali ibn Abi Talib and Mu'awiya ibn Abi Sufyan, the governor of Syria.

            226

            The Mosque of Prophet ﷺ was damaged due to fire in the reign of ______?

            Correct Answer: Motasim Billah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Abu Isḥaq Muḥammad ibn Harun al-Rashid, better known by his regnal name al-Muʿtaṣim biʾllāh, was the eighth Abbasid caliph, ruling from 833 until his death in 842.

            A younger son of Caliph Harun al-Rashid, he rose to prominence through his formation of a private army composed predominantly of Turkic slave-soldiers.
            227

            Hazrat Bilal (R.A) was liberated by Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A), Amir bin Faheera was set free by ______?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Amir bin Fuhairah (RA) was a slave who accepted Islam and faced persecution in Makkah.
          • He was freed by Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA).
          • Later he served as his companion during the migration to Madinah.
            • 228

            Ajnadayn is located in ______?

            Correct Answer: Palestine
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Ajnadayn is the site of a famous battle fought in 634 AD between the forces of the Rashidun Caliphate and the Byzantine Empire.
          • It is located near the present-day city of Ramallah, Palestine.
            • 229

            The story of Habil and Qabil is mentioned in which Surah of the Holy Quran?

            Correct Answer: Surah e Maida
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Qabil and Habil are the first two sons of Hazrat Adam (AS) and Bibi Hawwa.

            The story of Habil and Qabil is mentioned in Surah e Maida.

            Qabil killed Habil, becoming the first murderer on the earth

            ***

            230

            The miracle of a pregnant female camel was sent to the nation of _____?

            Correct Answer: Samood
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The She-Camel was a miraculous female camel sent by God to the people of Samood in Al-Hijr, after they demanded a miracle from the Hazrat Saleh (AS).
            • Nation of Samood means قوم ثمود
            231

            Name the mosque which Holy Prophet (PBUH) visited in the event of Mairaj?

            Correct Answer: Bait-ul-Muqaddas
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The mosque visited by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) during the event of Mairaj is Bait-ul-Muqaddas (Al-Aqsa Mosque).

            Bait-ul-Muqaddas (in Jerusalem) is where the Prophet (PBUH) led all the previous prophets in prayer during the Night Journey (Isra).

            From there, he ascended to the heavens (Mairaj).

            232

            Where was Hazrat Yousaf (AS) sold as a slave?

            Correct Answer: Egypt
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Important Question about "Hazrat Yousaf (AS)

            Prophet Yusuf (عليه السلام) was one of the twelve sons of Prophet Yaqub (AS).

            His mother's name was Raheel.

            Hazrat Yousaf (AS) remained in the well for three days.

            27 times Hazrat Yousuf was mentioned in the Holy Quran. 

            Hazrat Yousaf (AS) was sold as a slave in Egypt.

            Zulaikha bought Hazrat Yousaf (AS)

            The real brother of Hazrat Yousaf A.S was Bin Yameen.

            Hazrat Yousaf's (AS) talent for interpreting dreams.

            Palestine is the grave of Hazrat Yousaf (علیہ).

            ******

            233

            What is the number of Rajab in Islamic calendar?

            Correct Answer: 7
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Rajab is the seventh month of the Islamic calendar. 

            Isra and Miraj (Muhammad's journey from Mecca to Jerusalem and then through the seven Heavens) took place in Rajab.


            Must remember all 12 Islamic months name

            List of Islamic Months

            1. Muḥarram, محرم الحرام
            2. Ṣafar, صفر المظفر
            3. Rabi al Awwal ربیع الاول
            4. Rabi al Sani ربیع الثانی
            5. Jumada al Awwal جمادی الاول
            6. Jumada al Thani جمادی الثانی
            7. Rajab, رجب المرجب
            8. Shabanشعبان المعظم
            9. Ramadan(fasting), رمضان المبارک
            10. Shawwal شوال المکرم
            11. Dhu al Qadah ذوالقعدۃ الحرام
            12. Dhu al Hijjah ذوالحجۃ الحرام:

            Each month begins approximately at the time of the new moon.

            234

            How many times Holy Prophet (PBUH) went to Allah with the request to reduce the number of Salats until these remained only five?

            Correct Answer: 5 times
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Initially, Allah commanded 50 daily prayers, but after the Prophet's (PBUH) repeated requests, the number was gradually reduced to 5.

            This event occurred during the Isra and Mi'raj (Night Journey and Ascension), a significant miracle in Islamic history.

            235

            Which Muslim country is a member of NATO?

            Correct Answer: Turkey
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            Know about NATO

            Know about NATO

            NATO full form: North Atlantic Treaty Organization

            Founded: 4 April, 1949 (Washington, USA)

            Headquarters: Brussels, Belgium

            Total members: 32 (30 European & 2 North American countries)

            Latest Member: Sweden

            Type: Military Alliance

            Official Languages: English & French

            Founding members: 12

            Pakistan: Not a NATO member

            Member Muslim country: Turkiye  

            In April 2024, Sweden became 32nd member of NATO.

             

            List of NATO member countries

            1.       Albania

            2.       Belgium

            3.       Bulgaria

            4.       Canada

            5.       Croatia

            6.       Czechia

            7.       Denmark

            8.       Estonia

            9.       France

            10.   Germany

            11.   Greece

            12.   Hungary

            13.   Iceland

            14.   Italy

            15.   Latvia

            16.   Lithuania

            17.   Luxembourg

            18.   Montenegro

            19.   Netherlands

            20.   North Macedonia

            21.   Norway

            22.   Poland

            23.   Portugal

            24.   Romania

            25.   Slovakia

            26.   Slovenia

            27.   Spain

            28.   Turkiye

            29.   United Kingdom

            30.   United States

            31.   Finland

            32. Sweden 

            *****

            236

            The area between the minbar (pulpit) and the Grave of the Holy Prophet (SAW) is called?

            Correct Answer: Riadul Jannah
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Riadul Jannah (Garden of Paradise) is a sacred area inside Masjid al-Nabawi, between the minbar and the grave of Prophet Muhammad (SAW).
            • 237

            The payments after the death of a pilgrim are given to:

            Correct Answer: Close Relative
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            If a pilgrim passes away, any due payments, refunds, or compensation are handed over to their close relative as per Hajj policies.

            238

            Complaint, if not even relevant, according to the rules, should be:

            Correct Answer: Recorded
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Even if a complaint is irrelevant, it must still be recorded according to rules.
          • Recording complaints ensures transparency and accountability, allowing for future reference and potential review.
          • It prevents unfair dismissals and maintains a formal complaint tracking system.
            • 239

            On 10th Zilhaj, the ritual of Rami (stoning the devil) is performed at which Jamarat?

            Correct Answer: Jamarah Uqbah
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            On 10th Dhul Hijjah, pilgrims perform Rami al-Jamarat (stoning the devil) at Jamarah Uqbah (the largest pillar).


            It is performed on four consecutive days of the Hajj, the 10th, 11th, 12th, and 13th days of Dhul-Hijjah (the 12th Islamic Month).

            On the first day, pilgrims cast seven stones at Jamarat al-Aqaba.

            240

            Which company has the contract for traveling during Hajj?

            Correct Answer: Tawafh Company
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Tawafh Company is responsible for managing transportation for Hajj pilgrims in Saudi Arabia.
          • It ensures smooth bus services, logistics, and movement between Mina, Arafat, Muzdalifah, and Makkah.
            • 241

            After 9 PM, does anyone enter into the Hujjaj room?

            Correct Answer: Not Allowed
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • There are strict regulations regarding privacy and security in the Hajj accommodations.
          • After 9 PM, entry is restricted to ensure rest and order among pilgrims.
            • 242

            Hujjaj offer Eid-ul-Adha Prayer on which day?

            Correct Answer: No Eid prayer for Hujjaj
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Pilgrims (Hujjaj) do not offer Eid-ul-Adha prayer because they are engaged in Hajj rituals on the 10th of Dhul Hijjah.
          • Instead of the prayer, pilgrims perform Rami al-Jamarat (stoning of the devil), sacrifice an animal (Qurbani), and shave their heads.
            • 243

            Hujjaj can change their assigned rooms by?

            Correct Answer: Rooms cannot be changed
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Once a room is assigned during Hajj, it cannot be changed due to strict logistics and management.
          • This ensures fair accommodation distribution and avoids mismanagement.
            • 244

            Pilgrims can go through the Airports after?

            Correct Answer: Clearance from customs in airport
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Pilgrims must pass through customs clearance at the airport before departure.
          • Other processes like immigration, security checks, and document verification are also necessary but customs clearance is the final step before proceeding.
            • 245

            Where is OPAP (Office for Pilgrim Affairs of Pakistan) situated?

            Correct Answer: Azizia
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • OPAP (Office of Pilgrim Affairs of Pakistan) is located in Azizia, a district in Makkah, Saudi Arabia.
          • This office handles Hajj-related services, complaints, accommodations, and support for Pakistani pilgrims.
            • 246

            In case of medical illness, approach:

            Correct Answer: Pakistan Mission Hospital
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • During Hajj, Pakistan Mission Hospitals provide medical assistance to Pakistani pilgrims.
          • Other options like Hajj Operators or Pilgrims’ Affairs Offices may assist in arrangements but do not provide medical treatment.
            • 247

            Which day is called Yawm-e-Tarwiyah?

            Correct Answer: 8th Dul Hijjah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Yawm-e-Tarwiyah is the first day of Hajj

            It is the 8th day of Dhul-Hijjahr

            When pilgrims travel to Mina and prepare for the journey.

            The pilgrim put on his Ihraam after the Salaat of Zohr & Asr and head out of Makkah to Mina.

            He spends the rest of the day and night praying in Mina, preparing to set out to Arafaat.

            248

            How much affidavit does a woman needs to submit to go to Hajj alone

            Correct Answer: 50
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • According to the latest Hajj policies in many countries, including Pakistan, a woman aged 45 years or above can perform Hajj without a Mahram if she submits an affidavit stating that she will travel with an organized group.
          • The affidavit is usually on a Rs. 50 stamp paper confirming her consent and travel arrangements.
            • 249

            Which app is used to determine the correct route during hajj?

            Correct Answer: Google Maps
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Google Maps is a navigation app for determining routes and directions, making it the best choice for this question.
          • "Pak Hajj Guide" may provide information about Hajj but does not serve as a navigation tool.
            • 250

            A Welfare Staff can be a deputy against how many Hujjaj

            Correct Answer: 100
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            One welfare staff member manages 100 Hujjaj to ensure smooth operations during Hajj.

            251

            Which committee is responsible for reviewing complaints against HGOs?

            Correct Answer: Complaint Disposal Committee (CDC)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Complaint Disposal Committee (CDC) handles complaints against Hajj Group Organizers (HGOs) to ensure proper service quality and accountability.

            252

            What is the punishment for drug trafficking during Hajj in Saudi Arabia?

            Correct Answer: Death
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Saudi Arabia enforces strict drug laws, and drug trafficking is punishable by the death penalty.
            253

            If the complaint of Haji is not under the umbrella?

            Correct Answer: Record
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            If a complaint is not categorized under a specific policy, it still needs to be recorded for future reference

            254

            On which days of Dhul-Hijjah is the ritual of Jamarat performed?

            Correct Answer: 10th to 13th Dhul-Hijjah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Stoning of the Devil ritual is performed at the Jamarat,

            which is located in Mina, near Mecca.

            It is performed on four consecutive days of the Hajj, the 10th, 11th, 12th, and 13th days of Dhul-Hijjah (the 12th Islamic Month).

            On the first day, pilgrims cast seven stones at Jamarat al-Aqaba.

            255

            What color jackets are worn by Pakistani Muavineen (assistants) during Hajj?

            Correct Answer: Green
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Muavineen (assistants) from Pakistan who assist pilgrims during Hajj wear green jackets or coats to distinguish themselves and make it easier for pilgrims to identify and seek help from them.

            256

            What color bracelet is provided by the Saudi government to pilgrims during Hajj?

            Correct Answer: Yellow
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Saudi government provides yellow bracelets to pilgrims, often as a means of identification or for tracking purposes during the Hajj pilgrimage.

            257

            What is the 2023 act governing Hajj regulations called?

            Correct Answer: Act of Hajj and Umrah Regulation 2023
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The act governing Hajj regulations in Pakistan in 2023 is called the Hajj and Umrah (Regulation) Act, 2023.

            The act was approved by Parliament and will be implemented after the rules and regulations are formulated. 

            258

            What kind of action by the pilgrims, if abandoned, requires paying a penalty (دم)?

            Correct Answer: Wajib
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Suppose a pilgrim abandons a Wajib (mandatory) action during Hajj or Umrah.

            In that case, it requires a penalty (دم) to be paid, which usually involves sacrificing an animal or other compensatory actions.

            259

            What do you say when you ask someone about a medical store?

            Correct Answer: أريد الذهاب إلى صيدلية لشراء الدواء
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             "أريد الذهاب إلى صيدلية لشراء الدواء" means "I want to go to a pharmacy to buy medicine,"

            260

            Keep the maximum weight of personal luggage within the specified limit upon returning from Hajj?

            Correct Answer: 7 kg
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            261

            Pakistani Haji will request prior permission through which app for their turn in Riyaz Al-Jannah?

            Correct Answer: NUSUK App
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            262

            In case of arrival through Jeddah airport, it has to be handed over to the representative of the Maktab?

            Correct Answer: Passport
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The passport is handed over to the representative of the Maktab upon arrival at Jeddah airport for identification and administrative purposes.

            263

            What is the status of the Sai between Safa and Marwah?

            Correct Answer: Wajib
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sa'i between Safa and Marwah is an essential rite (wajib)


            Running b/w Safa & Marwa seven times is called Sayee. 

            Muslims travel back and forth between them 

            It is an integral part of the Hajj and Umrah in Islam.

            After performing Say’ee Hujjaj go to Mina.

            **

            It is done to symbolize Hajar's (Wive of Prophet Ibraheem, Peace be upon them both) search for water for her son Prophet Ismael (Peace be upon Him).

            264

            What is the status of the Tawaf al-Wida (Farewell Tawaf)?

            Correct Answer: Wajib
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            It is a farewell Tawaf and is performed as a final ritual of Hajj when a pilgrim leaves Makkah.

            It is wajib.

            To perform Tawaf Al-Wada, Muslims are directed to circumambulate the Holy Kaaba seven times and pray two Rakats (only if one wants to, as it is not mandatory).

            265

            What is the largest Mosque in Mina?

            Correct Answer: Masjid e Khayf
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Al Khaif Mosque is situated in Mina 
            • It is largest mosque in Mina
            • Where pilgrims offer their prayers during their stay at Mina.
            • It is 25,000 square meters in area and can accommodate 25,000 pilgrims.
            266

            When do the pilgrims depart for Arafat?

            Correct Answer: 9 Dhul Hajj
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pilgrims depart for Arafat on the morning of the 9th day of Dhul-Hijjah,

            It is the second day of the Hajj pilgrimage. 


            The Day of Arafah is the most important day of the Hajj. 

            Pilgrims leave Mina for Arafat after performing the Fajr prayer. 

            267

            Building Duty Officer is responsible for promptly dispatching unidentified goods to?

            Correct Answer: Lost and Found Cell
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Building Duty Officer is responsible for dispatching unidentified goods to the Lost and Found Cell, 


            The Lost and Found Centre of the Pakistan Hajj Mission, Makkah has successfully returned over 317 missing items, including bags, wheelchairs, mobile phones

            268

            As per the policy, a pilgrim is allowed to perform Ibadah in Riyazul Jannah:

            Correct Answer: Nusuk application appointment
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            To enter Riyazul Jannah, you must book a slot on the Nusuk app, which is the official Saudi Arabian app for managing Hajj and Umrah permits


            269

            Which authority is responsible for ensuring the safety of pilgrims in Mina?

            Correct Answer: Saudi Civil Defense
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             The General Directorate of Civil Defense raised the level of readiness in Mina to receive pilgrims, adopting several measures to maintain their safety.

            270

            What is the Arabic term for railway station?

            Correct Answer: Mahattah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             محطة سكة حديد

            railway station

            271

            Which app is not used for call in Saudi Arabia?

            Correct Answer: Whatsapp
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            272

            How many hours in advance should the notice for buses to Medina be posted?

            Correct Answer: 18-24 Hours
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            273

            Where is the Main Control Office (MCO) to facilitate the Pakistani pilgrims situated?

            Correct Answer: Makkah
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            The Main Control Office (MCO) in Makkah

            It is a Hajj operation control center that manages the Hajj operation for pilgrims from Pakistan.

            The MCO is operated by the Hajj Organizers Association of Pakistan (HOAP) and the Office of Pilgrims Affairs Pakistan (OPAP). 

            274

            What is the shape of the Hateem?

            Correct Answer: Half-circle
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Hateem, also known as Hijr Ismail

            Shape: Semi-circular

            Location: Opposite the northwest wall of the Kaaba

            Significance: Considered part of the Kaaba's original structure pilgrims do not walk within the space between the Hateem and the Kaaba during Tawa

            275

            What does Yawm-e-Tarwiyah refer to?

            Correct Answer: The day when pilgrims travel to Mina and prepare for Hajj
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Yawm-e-Tarwiyah is the first day of Hajj

            It is the 8th day of Dhul-Hijjahr

            When pilgrims travel to Mina and prepare for the journey.

            The pilgrim put on his Ihraam after the Salaat of Zohr & Asr and head out of Makkah to Mina.

            He spends the rest of the day and night praying in Mina, preparing to set out to Arafaat.


            276

            Which of the following is not within the scope of the division's concern under the Hajj Act 2023?

            Correct Answer: Supervising financial accounts of HGOs
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Supervising financial accounts of HGOs is not within the scope of the division as per the Hajj Act 2023.

            277

            What are most obligatory period of Hajj?

            Correct Answer: Waqoof e Arfat
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Waqoof e Arfat is the most obligatory  period of Hajj

            The Day of Arafat, or Yawm Al-Waqf, is the second day of the Hajj pilgrimage to Makkah. 

            It is the ninth day of Dhul-Hijjah, the final month of the Islamic calendar. 

            Pilgrims stand at Arafat from midday (Dhuhr) until sunset. 



            There are three obligatory acts of Hajj, known as the pillars of Hajj: 

            1. Ihram
            2. WUF
            3. Tawaf al-Ifadah
            278

            The fifth day of Hajj is called?

            Correct Answer: 12 Dhul Hajj
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            (5th day of Hajj – 12th Dhu'l-Hijjah) :

            Repeat Ramy Al Jamarat.

            Return to Al Haram for the farewell Tawaf.

            Spend the night in Makkah or Mina

            279

            Under the Act 2023, can the Federal Government be held liable for actions taken by a government officer acting in good faith?

            Correct Answer: No, the Federal Government is protected from liability if actions are taken in good faith.
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • The Act 2023 provides immunity to the Federal Government from liability if the actions taken by an officer are in good faith.
          • This legal principle ensures that government officials can perform their duties without fear of being sued, provided they act with honesty and within legal boundaries.
          • The correct answer is B, meaning the government is not liable for actions taken in good faith.






            • 280

            An extension of _____days can be given to welfare staff beyond 45 days stay in KSA:

            Correct Answer: Extensions are not permitted except in exceptional cases
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Extension in tenure of welfare staff beyond 45 days stay in KSA sha[ not be granted welfare to any staff

            281

            Pilgrims should be cautious while crossing roads, as traffic runs in a specific direction in Saudi Arabia?

            Correct Answer: Right Hand
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            سعودی عرب میں ٹریفک دائیں ہاتھ چلتی ہے لہذا سرک عبور کرتے وقت اس بات کا  خاص خیال رکھیں

            282

            What is the meaning of Zarb-e-Azb?

            Correct Answer: Sharp & Cutting Strike
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            • Zarb-e Azab. 'Azb' refers to one of the seven swords of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) that he carried along in the Ghazwaz of Uhad and Badr to strike hard infidels.
            • Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad Launched on February 22, 2017,
            • the intensive nationwide operation against terror groups steered
            • by Army Chief General Qamar Javid Bajwa
            • Operation Zarb-e-Azb started by Army Staff General Raheel Sharif 
            • Operation Zarb-e-Azb start from 15 June 2014 till 22 February 2017
            • Location: Pakistan, North Waziristan Agency, Federally Administered Tribal Area, Karachi, Balochistan, Punjab
            • RK11-12-22
            283

            The reward of which prayer is equal to the reward of Hajj or Umrah?

            Correct Answer: Namaz-i-Ishraq
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Ishraq prayer is an optional prayer, or salah, that is done about 15-20 minutes after the sun rises.

            Before you go to bed, research what time the sun will rise in your city for the next day, and set an alarm for that time.

            If you lose track of time, just look at the position of the sun over the horizon.
            284

            Which Prophet earned his living by making wallets?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Suilaiman (AS)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sulaymān ibn Dāwūd was, according to the Quran, a Malik and Nabī of the Israelites. Islamic tradition generally holds that he was the third King of Jewish people, and wise ruler for the nation. 
            285

            In which year were the vowels inserted in the Qur’an?

            Correct Answer: 43 Hijri
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Vowels was introduced by hijaz bin Marwan.

            Vowels was Inserted in Quran by Hijaz bin Yousaf.
            286

            The first Islamic institution was ______?

            Correct Answer: Masjid-e-Quba
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The first Islamic institution was Masjid-e-Quba.

            It was built in the 7th century CE.

            The Islamic Prophet Muhammad SAW established it while migrating from Makkah to Medina. 

            287

            What does Taraweeh means?

            Correct Answer: To Rest
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            Taraweeh means To Rest

            Taraweeh prayer is performed in congregation during Ramadan after Isha and before Witr

            Tarawih prayers are special Muslim prayers involving reading long portions of the Quran

            Tarawih is prayed in sets of two rakyats each
            ND2023-2-12
            288

            Interest was prohibited in ______ A.H.

            Correct Answer: 8th
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Interest is prohibited in Islam as it appears explicitly in the Holy Qur'an and the Sunnah of the Prophet.

            Interest was prohibited in 8th A.H.

            ****

            RY30012023




            289

            Naqeeb-e-Islam is the title of ______?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            21 Ramzan 40th Hijri Fajar prayer to the mosque of Kufa Ibn -e- Muljim.

            Hazrat Ali was killed in the mosque of Kufa by the sword of one of the Kharijites, Ibn Muljam Moradi.

            Buried outside the city of Kufa.

            His shrine and the city of Najaf were built around his tomb.

            290

            The first biography on the life of Holy Prophet ( PBUH) is written by _____?

            Correct Answer: Ibn-e-Ishaq
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The first biography of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, Life of God's Messenger,

            was written by Ibn Ishaq around 767 CE (150 AH).


            **Additional Information***

            The original text of Sīrat Rasūl Allāh by Ibn Ishaq is lost,

            but much of it was copied into the work of Ibn Hisham, who died in 833 AD (218 AH).

            Other editors, such as Ibn Jarir al-Tabari, also helped preserve Ibn Ishaq's work

            291

            In which battle Hazrat Zaid bin Haris was martyred?

            Correct Answer: Mutah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Battle of Mu'tah in September 629, where Zayd was killed.

            Hazrat Zaid bin Haris the adopted son of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

            Hazrat Zaid bin Haris  is commonly regarded as the fourth person to have accepted Islam

            Zayd Bin Harith's (R.A) name is mentioned in the Al-Ahzab 33rd Surah of the Quran 

            Hazrat Zaid was the first adult male to become a Muslim and the only Muslim apart from Muhammad to be mentioned by name in the Quran

            The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of Hub Rasool-Ullah to Hazrat Usama bin Zaid R.A 

            ND21-2-2023



            292

            When was the first mosque of Islam built?

            Correct Answer: 1 AH
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            The Quba Mosque is a mosque located on the outskirts of Medina

            The first stones of Quba Mosque were positioned by Muhammad he arrived on his emigration from the city of Mecca to Medina

            Muhammad spent 14 days in this mosque praying qaṣr

            this mosque is said to be where the first Friday prayer was held, led by Muhammad


            Very confusing question

            Masjid-ul-Haram is the first masjid on the surface of the earth.

            while

            Masjid-e-Quba  is the first masjid in history of Islam.

            ***

            Masjid quba is built after Hazrat Muhammad saw arrived in Madina.

            ****


            293

            In which country was the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) founded?

            Correct Answer: Morocco
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Organization was established upon a decision of the historical summit which took place in Rabat, Kingdom of Morocco on 12th Rajab 1389 Hijra
            • Its headquarters is in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia.
            • As the largest international organization outside the United Nations.
            • The OIC has 57 member countries.
            • The first summit was held in Rabat.
            • The official languages are Arabic, English, and French.
            294

            What do you say when you ask someone for help?

            Correct Answer: هل يمكنك مساعدتي
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            "هل يمكنك مساعدتي" means "Can you help me?" in Arabic.

            295

            When is Wuquf at Muzdalifah?

            Correct Answer: 9th Dhul-Hijjah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Day of Wuquf-e-Arafat, also known as the Day of 'Arafah, falls on the 9th day of Dhul-Hijjah


            296

            Ada Bin Hatam Tai embraced Islam in _____?

            Correct Answer: 9 Hijri
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Adi ibn Hatim al-Tai  was a leader of the Arab tribe of Tayy 

            Adi ibn Hatim al-Tai was one of the companions of Muhammad. 

            Adi ibn Hatim al-Tai embarrassed Islam in 9 Hijri

            Ada Bin Hatam Tai was the son of Hatim al-Tai, 

            ND18-5-2023

            297

            The Companions of Holy Prophet SAW at Hudabiya was ____?

            Correct Answer: 1400
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            It was a pivotal treaty between Muhammadﷺ, representing the state of Medina, and the Qurayshi tribe of Mecca in January 628 (Dhu al-Qi'dah, AH 6).
            298

            What was the important event in the month of 13th Nabvi?

            Correct Answer: Hijrat e Madina
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            •  Prophet Muhammad’s migration from Mecca to Yathrib (Medina)on September 24, 622.
            • With Hazrat Abu Bakar R.A Hijra from Makkah to Madina started in the 13th Nabvi year.
            • Hijri year starts from this event.
            299

            In the battle of Uhad Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) selected ____ Skillful archers to stay on the mountain

            Correct Answer: 50
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Abu Sufyan commander the infidels in  Uhad Ghazwa

            ***

            It was fought in 3 Hijri 

            Ohad is the name of the mountain

            3000 Infidels participated in the battle of Uhad

            Abdullah ibn Ubayy decided to withdraw his 300 men before the battle of Uhad. 

            The Battle of Uhud was fought on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD, in the valley north of Mount Uhud.

            1000 Muslims participated in the battle of Uhad

            3000 Infidels participated in the battle of Uhad Infidel means کفار
            In the battle of Uhad Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) selected 50Skillful archers to stay on the mountain

            *****

            ND13-1-2023


              300

              How many years after the birth of Holy Prophet (SAW), Hazrat Bibi Aamina R.A died?

              Correct Answer: Six years
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              v  After 6 years of the birth of Holy prophet Bibi Aamna died.

              v  Prophet Muhammad's father Abdullah ibn Abdul-Muttalib died before his birth,

              His paternal uncle Abu Talib later took care of him.

              301

              The color of the first Islamic flag was ______?

              Correct Answer: White
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The color of the Islamic flag is white

              the Prophet Muhammad used the white flag to represent both the leader of the Muslim army and the first Muslim state.


              Additional information

              The Umayyad dynasty (661–750) used white as their symbolic color as a reminder of Muhammad's first battle at Badr 

              The color of the Flag of the Islamic Army in Ghazwa Badr was Green, and Muslims won.

              ND-21-5-2023ND19112023

              302

              How many kings accepted Islam when Holy Prophet sent them letters?

              Correct Answer: 4
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Perumal is first king to accept Islam at the hands of Prophet Muhammad SAW.

              303

              Which of the following is called Year of Deputations?

              Correct Answer: 9 Hijri
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The year 9 Hijri (630 CE) is known as the "Year of Deputations" (عام الوفود, 'Am al-Wufud').

              Because numerous delegations came to meet the Prophet Muhammad SAW and declare their allegiance to Islam.
              304

              For ___ years Haleema took care of Prophet (PBUH).

              Correct Answer: 6
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Halima Sadia was an Arabic Beduin woman.

              Foster mother of prophet Muhammad (PBUH)

              Halimah was from the tribe of Sa'd, a subdivision of Hawazin.

              Hazrat Halimah and her husband were from the tribe of Sa'd.

              For 6 years Haleema took care of Prophet (PBUH)

              Died: 630 CE in Madina

              ****

              3 foster mothers of the Holy Prophet

              1.        Hazrat Halema Saadia (RA)    
              2.      Hazrat Khola  (RA)
              3.        Hazrat Suwaiba (RA)
              ND-21-5-2023
              305

              Banu Quraiza, Banu Nadir, and Banu Qainuqa were three tribes of the ______?

              Correct Answer: Jews
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              more than twenty Jewish clans indicate of which three were prominent-the Banu Nadir, the Banu Qaynuqa, and the Banu Qurayza

              The Banu Nadir, led by Kab ibn al-Ashraf and Huyayy ibn Akhtab

              the Banu Qurayza fought with the Aws

              the Banu Qaynuqa allied itself with the tribe of Khazraj.

              Banu Kainuka, Banu Kaynuka, Banu Qainuqa, Banu Qaynuqa was one of the three main Jewish tribes living in the 7th century of Medina,

              they were expelled during the Invasion of Banu Qaynuqa

               after breaking the treaty known as the Constitution of Medina

              ND5-2-2023
              306

              Ghar-e- Sor is mentioned in ________?

              Correct Answer: Surah Al Tauba
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Quran's Surah Al Tauba fortieth verse was revealed.
              • Also known as Ghar Thawr, the Cave of Thawr has religious significance.
              • This cave is where the Holy Prophet and his companion Abu Bakr al-Siddiq found shelter when fleeing from Quraish search parties.
              • Both resided in this cave for three days.
              • This event is revered among Muslims as miracles of God for Muhammad.
              •  غار ثور کہاں ہے: مکہ
              • آپ صَلَّی اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَلَیْہِ وَاٰلِہٖ وَسَلَّمَ کتنے دن غار ثور میں قیام کیا: تین دن
              • غار یار کس صحابی کو کہا جاتا ہے: حضرت ابو بکر رَضِیَ اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَنْہُ
              307

              How many Makki and Madni surah in Holy Quran?

              Correct Answer: 114
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              the number of Makki Surah is 86 

              the number of Madni is 28

              Madani Surahs revealed in Year = 10

              Makki Surahs revealed in Year = 13

              The Medni Surahs typically have more and longer ayat 

              The Makki Surahs typically have more and short ayat 


              Additional Information

              The number of verses in the Quran is 6,666.

              The total number of verses in Quraan is 6,236 excluding Bismillah and 6349 including Bismillah.

              The total number of Surah in the Holy Quran is 114

              There is Juz 30 in the Qur'an.

              There are  14 Sajda talawat in the Quran.

              D19-3-2023ND29-5-2023

              308

              What was the age of Hazrat Fatima (R.A) when the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) passed away?

              Correct Answer: 27 years
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Fatima died in 11/632, within six months of Hazrat Muhammad's death. 

              She was about eighteen or twenty-seven at that time according to Shia and Sunni sources, respectively.

              309

              The Gap between first and second Wahi was ______ months?

              Correct Answer: 2 Years 6 months
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              According to Mubarakpuri, the exact date of this event was Monday, the 21st of Ramadan just before sunrise, i.e. August 10, 610 C.E. – when Muhammad was 40 lunar years, 6 months and 12 days of age, i.e. 39 solar years, 3 months and 22 days.

              یادرکھیے

              کچھ جگہ پر جواب چھ ماہ بھی آتا ہے۔ تو پیپر میں اگر چھ ماہ یا دو سال چھ ماہ کا آپشن ہو تو دونوں ہی ٹھیک ہیں
              310

              How many times the word Mubeen is repeated in Surah Yasin?

              Correct Answer: 7
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The word Mubeen is repeated seven times in Surah Yasin (Chapter 36) of the Holy Quran.

              The word "Mubeen" is usually translated as "clear".

              There are 119 Aayaths in the Quran that contain the word "Mubeen".

              311

              Sixth Wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ was _______?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Salmah (RA)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Hazrat Salma was the 6th wife of Hazrat Muhammad 

              She was 26 years old at the time of Nikah and Muhammad ﷺ was 56 years ago

              "Umm Salama" was her kunya meaning, "mother of Salama".

              Before marriage to Muhammad, she was married to Abu Salama Abd (foster brother of Prophetﷺ)

              She had four children from Abu Salama. 


              Additional Information:

              Nabi e Akram (ﷺ) had eleven (11) wives also known as “Azwaaj e Mutahharaat”

              312

              Who suggested the name "Ahmed" for Holy Prophet (PBUH)?

              Correct Answer: Hazrat Amina
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The name "Ahmed" was suggested by Hazrat Amina (R.A) while the name "Muhammad" was suggested by Hazrat Abdul Mutalib.

              313

              How many Surahs name is only one letter?

              Correct Answer: Three
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Three Sorahs in Quran that starts only one letter:
                             Surah 50 — qaaf (ق)
               Surah 38 — saad (              ص).
              Surah 68 — nune (              ن).

              314

              Hazrat Fatima died how many months after the death of her father Muhammad (PBUH)?

              Correct Answer: 6 months
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Fatima died in 11/632, within six months of Muhammad's death.

              She was buried in Jannat-ul-Baqi

              She is 4th daughter and 5th child of Hazrat Muhammad and Hazrat Khadija (RA)

              She was born in Makkah on the 20th of Jumada al-Thaniya 18 B.H.

              She married Hazrat Ali RA ( 4th Rashidun Caliphs) in 2 AH

              She had 2 sons and 2 daughters: 

              1. Imam Hussain
              2. Imam Hassan
              3. Zaynab 
              4. Umm Kulthum

              Title of Bibi Fatima is: Tahira, Zahra, Umm Abiha, Sayyidatu nisa-alamin and al-Batul


              315

              How many uncles of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W) embraced Islam?

              Correct Answer: 2
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • Two uncles of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W), Hazrat Hamza and Hazrat Abbas, embraced Islam.
            • Hazrat Hamza embraced Islam in the sixth Nabvi year (616 CE).
              • 316

              Where did Prophet (SAW) held his court?

              Correct Answer: Masjid-e-Nabvi
              Show explanation
              Explanation
            • Prophet Muhammad (SAW) held his court and conducted state affairs in Masjid-e-Nabvi in Madinah.
            • It served as a center for worship, education, and governance during his time.
              • 317

              Which areas are referred to as Mashair?

              Correct Answer: Mina, Arafat, and Muzdalifah
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Mashaer (Holy sites in Makkah including Mina, Muzdalifah and Arafat)

              The area is divided into seven Establishments 

              Each Establishment has its camps, routes and parking spaces to handle the movement of pilgrims 

              Where pilgrims should make a Dua after they have slept in Muzdalifah while they are going to Makkah to throw Jamrat al-Aqabah on the morning of the 10th of Dhul-Hijjah.

              318

              According to which document is the MORA&IH mandated to make Hajj arrangements?

              Correct Answer: Government Rules of Business 1973
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               The Government, Rules of Business 1973 mandates the MORA&IH to handle Hajj arrangements.

               MORA&IH stands for Ministry of Religious Affairs & Interfaith Harmony

              319

              The physical and financial capacity of a Muslim for performing Hajj is called?

              Correct Answer: Sahib-e-Istitaat
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The term "Sahib-e-Istitaat" refers to a Muslim who meets the physical and financial prerequisites for performing Hajj

              Hajj is one of the five pillars of Islam 

              It is mandatory, once-in-a-lifetime


              Additional information 

              Verbal meaning of Hajj is The will to visit

              There are 3 Farz, 10 Sunnat and 6 Wajibat in Hajj

                Farz in Hajj are Three (3)

                Farz in Umrah are Four (4)

                There are three different types of Hajj namely:
                Hajj-ul-Ifrad.
                Hajj-ul-Qiran.
                Hajj-ul-Tamattu.

                320

                How often is Hajj mandatory for Muslims?

                Correct Answer: Once in a lifetime
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                 Hajj is a pilgrimage that Muslims are required to perform at least once in their lifetime

                It is performed during the month of Dhul Hijjah

                It usually takes place from the 8th to the 12th day of Dhul Hijjah

                321

                Whose rights are given in Quran majeed with rights of relatives

                Correct Answer: Neighbours
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Quran mentions the rights of neighbors alongside the rights of relatives. 

                Surah An-Nisa (4:36) highlights the importance of being good to neighbors and those close to you

                322

                Which is the most important belief after Tawhid?

                Correct Answer: Risalat
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                the most important belief after Tawhid (the oneness of God) is "Risalah"


                Risalah, which means "message" or "prophethood

                After Tawheed (belief in the oneness of Allah) and risalat (messengers) is salah (it is regular or ritual prayer).


                 توحید کے بعد ایمان کے دوسرے رکن کا تعلق فرشتوں پر ایمان سے ہے۔


                 ایمان کے چھ ارکان (ارکانِ ایمان):


                اللہ پر ایمان (توحید)  

                فرشتوں پر ایمان   

                 اللہ کی کتابوں پر ایمان  

                 اللہ کے رسولوں پر ایمان  

                 قیامت کے دن پر ایمان  

                 تقدیر (اچھی یا بری) پر ایمان

                323

                The tax collected from prisoner is called _____?

                Correct Answer: None of these
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                1. Jizya: A tax historically levied on non-Muslims living under an Islamic state, in exchange for protection and exemption from military service. It has no connection to prisoners.

                2. Fidya: Compensation for failing to fulfill certain obligations like fasting. This is unrelated to imprisonment.

                3. Ashar: A tithe (1/10th tax) on agricultural produce, unrelated to prisoners.

                In Islam, prisoners are generally entitled to humane treatment and are not burdened with taxes for their captivity.

                If there are costs incurred by a prisoner (e.g., restitution for crimes), this would be part of the judicial process and not termed as a "tax."

                324

                Which Sahabi made the ring of Holy Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W)?

                Correct Answer: Hazrat Ya'ala bin Ummyah (R.A)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Hazrat Ya'ala bin Ummyah (R.A.) made the ring of Holy Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W).

                The ring was used by the Prophet SAW for sealing documents and correspondences.

                325

                What is the name of Hadis, which is according to the Holy Quran and reason?

                Correct Answer: Hadith Sahih
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Hadith Sahih refers to a saying or action of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) that is verified to be authentic and trustworthy.

                It is considered the most reliable type of Hadith, as it is based on a continuous chain of narration from the Prophet SAW himself.

                326

                Name the woman, after Hazrat Khadija (RA) who embraced Islam?

                Correct Answer: Hazrat Labala bint Haris
                Show explanation
                Explanation
              • Lubaba bint Haris (R.A) was the second woman to convert to Islam, on the same day as Khadijah (R.A).
              • Lubaba belonged to the Hilal tribe.
                • 327

                The commandment of “Qazaf punishment” was given in ______?

                Correct Answer: 5 AH
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The commandment of "Qazaf punishment" (false accusation of adultery) was revealed in 5 AH in Surah An-Nur.

                Qazaf is Clause in Islam

                Qazf in Islam is imposed on: False accusations of Zina

                Had–e–Qazaf (false Accusation) is: 80 Lashes

                Lashes mean: کوڑے

                First Muslim who faced the first-ever ordinance of Qazf: Hasan ibn Sabit, Mustah ibn Asasa, Hamnah ibn Jahsh

                328

                نمازخسوف ــــــــــــــــــ کے وقت ادا کی جاتی ہے۔

                Correct Answer: چاند گرہن
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                نماز خسوف چاند گرہن کے وقت ادا کی جاتی ہے

                329

                Who is called Jami-ul-Quran?

                Correct Answer: Usman Ghani (R.A)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                ·         Hazrat Usman (R.A) is known as Jami-ul-Quran (compiler of Quran).

                He was the third caliph of Islam.

                330

                ماں کے قدموں تلے جنت ہے۔ یہ کیا ہے؟

                Correct Answer: ارشاد نبویﷺ
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                "جنت ماں کے قدموں تلے ہے" حضور نبی اکرم ﷺ کا ایک مشہور ارشاد ہے جو ماں کی عظمت اور احترام پر زور دیتا ہے۔
                یہ حدیث نبویﷺ کا مفہوم ہے، نہ کہ قرآن کی آیت۔







                331

                Who was the first caller to the prayers of Islam?

                Correct Answer: Hazrat Bilal (RA)
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Hazrat Bilal called the first Azan of  Fajar prayer

                First Azan was called out in 1 A.H

                Bilal ibn Rabah is considered to have been the first muezzin, chosen by Hazrat Muhammad himself.

                The Title of Bilal ibn Rabah: Sayyid al-Muʾaḏḏin

                Bilal ibn Rabah Born: 580 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabiais

                Bilal ibn Rabah Died: on March 2, 640 AD, in Damascus, Syria

                Bilal ibn Rabah's Place of burial: Bab el Saghir Cemetery, Damascus, Syria


                while 

                Hazrat 'Umar was the first person to suggest the calling of the Azan.

                ND23-6-2023


                332

                To which country did Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) have his first business trip?

                Correct Answer: Syria
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Prophet's first business trip was to Syria at age 12 with his uncle Abu Talib (RA).
                  • During this journey, he met a monk named Bahira in Basra, Syria. 
                  • Shab-e-Miraj refers to a separate miraculous night journey of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH
                  333

                  What is the meaning of the word “Quran”?

                  Correct Answer: That which is to be recited
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The meaning of the word "Quran" is "That which is to be recited."

                  It comes from the Arabic root "qara'a," meaning to read or recite.

                  This emphasizing its nature as a book to be recited aloud.
                  334

                  Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) passed away in which Hijri year?

                  Correct Answer: 11 AH
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Prophet Muhammad died on Monday at the age of 63, 12 Rabi' al-Awwal 11 AH, which corresponds to June 8, 632 CE.

                  Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) Born on c. 570 CE (53 BH) Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia.

                  335

                  Ameer Hamza (R.A) was martyred in __________ Ghazwa.

                  Correct Answer: Uhad
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Hazrat Hamza Died: on 22 March 625; Mount Uhud, Medina, Arabia

                  what is the relationship of Hazrat Hamza With Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)?

                  Answer: Uncle

                  ***

                  When did Hazrat Hamza embrace Islam?
                  Fifth Nabvi
                  ***
                  Ameer Hamza (R.A) was martyred in __________ Ghazwa.
                  Uhad
                  ***

                  Hamza ibn Abdul Muttalib Paternal Uncle of 

                  Hazrat Muhammad.

                  336

                  Cave of Ashab-e-Kahaf located in which country?

                  Correct Answer: Turkey
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation


                  The word "kahf" means "the cave in the mountain".

                  the sura is named "kahf" because it speaks about the story of the Companions of the Cave

                  Ashab e Kahf slept for 309 years

                  The number of Ashab-e-Kahf was 7

                  The story of Ashab al-Kahf is narrated in the eighteenth chapter of the Quran, called “al-Kahf”  In verses 9-26

                  سورہ کہف میں اصحاب کہف کے ساتھ کتا جانور کا ذکر آیا ہے

                  Qitmeer is the name of the dog in Surah Kahf.

                  ND10-3-2023


                  337

                  کس جلیل القدر صحابی کے لیے حضرت محمد صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم نے دعا فرمائی کہ وہ ایمان لے آئیں؟

                  Correct Answer: حضرت عمر فاروق
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  حضرت محمد ﷺ نے دعا فرمائی تھی:
                  "یا اللہ! عمر بن خطاب یا ابو جہل بن ہشام میں سے جو تجھے زیادہ محبوب ہو، اس کے ذریعے اسلام کو قوت عطا فرما۔"
                  اللہ تعالیٰ نے حضرت عمر فاروق رضی اللہ عنہ کی دعا قبول فرمائی، اور وہ ایمان لے آئے، جس سے اسلام کو بڑی تقویت ملی

                  338

                  In which Surah, Allah has mentioned his bounties the most?

                  Correct Answer: Surah Rehman
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                • Surah Ar-Rahman (Chapter 55) emphasizes Allah's numerous favors and infinite mercy.
                • The phrase "Which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?" is repeated 31 times.
                • Allah highlighting His countless blessings for humans and jinn.
                  • 339

                  Which Roman governor ordered the execution of Hazrat Essa (AS)?

                  Correct Answer: Politus
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                • Pontius Pilate: Roman governor of Judaea (26–36 CE) under Emperor Tiberius; ordered Jesus' crucifixion.
                • Hazrat Essa (AS): Known in Islam as Isa Ibn Maryam, born c. 4 BCE in Judea, Roman Empire.
                  • 340

                  The Holy Prophet SAW performed Hajjat-ul-Wada in _____?

                  Correct Answer: 10th Hijri
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The Holy Prophet (SAW) performed Hajjat-ul-Wida in the 10th Hijri.
                  • It was performed on Friday, the 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah (March 6, 632 A.D).
                  • This final pilgrimage included his renowned Farewell Sermon.
                  341

                  What was the religion of the forefathers of the Holy Prophet (SAW)?

                  Correct Answer: Deen-e-Ibraheemi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                • The religion of the forefathers of the Holy Prophet (SAW) was Deen-e-Ibraheemi.
                • It followed the monotheistic teachings of Prophet Ibrahim (AS).
                  • 342

                  Ghazwa Abwa was also known as ____ ?

                  Correct Answer: Ghazwa Widdan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  The Battle of Al-Abwah also known as the Battle of Waddan.
                  It was the first military expedition organized by the Prophet (ﷺ) to disrupt the trade routes between Makkah and Syria.
                  These caravan raids between 623 and 624 led to the Battle of Badr.
                  343

                  Mughal Emperor Akbar enforced Din-I-Ilahi in ______?

                  Correct Answer: 1582
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  Mughal emperor Akbar enforced Din-i-Ilahi in 1582.


                  ****

                  Akbar was born: 1542

                  Akbar died: in 1605

                  Mughal Emperor Akbar was born in:Sindh

                  Akbar was the ruler Mughal emperor from 1556 to 1605.

                  Mughal Emperor Akbar is buried: Agra

                  Mughal Emperor Akbar was the third Emperor from Mughal Family
                  344

                  Every prayer is preceded by an Azam with the exception of _____?

                  Correct Answer: All of them
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                • Funeral prayer, Eid-ul-Fitr, and Eid-ul-Azha prayers are not preceded by an Azan or Iqamah.
                • These prayers are unique in their practice and do not require the traditional call to prayer.
                  • 345

                  Name the prayer in which there is no change in Farz Rakaats in cast of Qasr?

                  Correct Answer: Maghrib
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In the case of Qasr (shortened prayer for travelers), there is no change in the number of Farz rakaats for Fajr, Maghrib, and Witr prayers.

                  Qasr refers to the practice of shortening the prayer when traveling over long distances.

                  It involves shortening the Farz components of Dhuhr, Asr, and Isha prayers to two Rakats.

                  346

                  Prophet Muhammed (PBHU) performed a total of ______ Umrahs.

                  Correct Answer: Four
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  The Holy Prophet SAW performed only 1 Hajj in the 10th A.H and 4 Umrahs:
                  1. First Umrah (6th Hijri): Resulted in the Treaty of Hudaibiya.
                  2. Second Umrah (7th Hijri): As set out in the Treaty of Hudaibiya.
                  3. Third Umrah (8th Hijri): After returning from the Battle of Hunain.
                  4. Fourth Umrah (10th Hijri) along with the Hajj, known as Hujjat al-Wada.
                  347

                  In which surah Quran name of Hazrat Zaid Bin Haris is mentioned?

                  Correct Answer: Al-Ahzab
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                    • The Battle of Mu'tah in September 629, where Zayd was killed.
                    Hazrat Zaid bin Haris the adopted son of the Islamic Prophet Muhammad.

                    Hazrat Zaid bin Haris  is commonly regarded as the fourth person to have accepted Islam

                    Zayd Bin Harith's (R.A) name is mentioned in the Al-Ahzab 33rd Surah of the Quran 

                    Hazrat Zaid was the first adult male to become a Muslim and the only Muslim apart from Muhammad to be mentioned by name in the Quran

                    The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave the title of Hub Rasool-Ullah to Hazrat Usama bin Zaid R.A 

                    ND21-2-2023



                  348

                  Which Prophet remained in the abdomen of a fish?

                  Correct Answer: Hazrat Younas AS
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Prophet who remained 40 days in the abdomen of a fish is Hazrat Younas AS.

                  He was swallowed by a giant fish (or whale) and remained there for a period of time.

                  Surah Younas is the 10th chapter (surah) of the Quran and has 109 verses (ayat).

                  Hazrat Younas AS is also known as Dhun-Nun.

                  He is mentioned in the Holy Quran four times.

                  349

                  What was the relation between Hazrat Haroon and Hazrat Musa (AS)?

                  Correct Answer: Brothers
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Hazrat Musa (Kaleemullah) and Hazrat Haroon (A.S) were brothers and prophets of God.
                    • Hazrat Musa (A.S) is buried in Ohud, KSA.
                    • Hazrat Haroon (A.S) is buried in Jordan.
                    • Hazrat Haroon (A.S) met the Holy Prophet ﷺ on the 5th heaven.
                    • Zubaida, wife of Haroon ur Rasheed.
                    • She was the mother of Al-Ameen and Al-Haroon.
                    350

                    Which Companion of the Holy Prophet SAW is known “Babul Ilm”?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali R.A
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hazrat Ali (R.A) was martyred on 21 Ramzan 40th Hijri Fajar prayer in the mosque of Kufa.

                    He is buried outside the city of Kufa, Iraq.

                    351

                    Cave of Thawr is a cave in a mountain _____?

                    Correct Answer: At the outskirts of Makkah
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Cave of Thawr is located in a mountain on the outskirts of Makkah.

                    It is where the Holy Prophet (PBUH) and Abu Bakr (RA) sought refuge during their migration to Medina (622 C.E).

                    This event is mentioned in Surah At-Tawbah (9:40).

                    352

                    Hazrat Umar R.A embraced Islam, influenced by which Surah of Holy Quran?

                    Correct Answer: Surah Taha
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The verses recited before Hazrat Umar (R.A) were from Surah Taha
                    • Embraced by Islam in 616 AD at the age of 32 years.
                    • He later became the second Caliph (634–644 AD).
                    • He belonged to the Banu Adi clan of Quraysh.

                    Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

                    353

                    The construction of Masjid-e-Nabvi started in ____?

                    Correct Answer: 1 Hijiri
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Al-Masjid an-Nabawi is the second mosque built by the Islamic prophet Muhammad in Medina

                    مسجد نبوی is a mosque in the city of Madina in the Saudi region of the Hejaz

                    مسجد نبوی the second largest mosque and the holiest site in Islam

                    The construction of مسجد نبوی started in: 1 Hijir

                    مسجد نبوی was built by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) himself, next to the house in 622 AD (first Hijri).

                    On 29th Hijrah was مسجد نبوی expanded

                    Height: 104 m

                    Burials: Abu Bakr, Umar

                    Minaret(s): 10

                    ND13-5-2023
                    354

                    Who gave Hazrat Abu Bakar the idea to collect the Quran in the form of a book?

                    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    At the suggestion of Hazrat Umar (RA), Hazrat Abu Bakr ( R.A) entrusted the task of compilation of the holy Quran to Zaid bin Sabit (RA).
                    Abu Bakr was a sahabi, one of the companions of Muhammad and the first Caliph of Islam.
                    He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.
                    His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya.
                    Hazrat Usman gave the idea to Hazrat Abu Bakar to collect the Quran in the form of a book
                    Holy Quran Collect in the reign of Hazrat Abubakar RA was done by Hazrat Zaid RA between 11 to 14 Hijri (732-34)
                    ND-21-5-2023


                    355

                    In which Battle Abu Jahl was killed?

                    Correct Answer: Badr
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Amr ibn Hishām al-Makhzumi also called Abū Jahl.

                    Abu Jahl was fatally and badly wounded by Mu'awwidh ibn 'Afrā' and Mu'ādh ibn 'Amr ibn al-Jamūḥ and eventually killed by Abdullah ibn Masud on March 13, 624.

                    ***

                    Battle of Badr:

                    The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam.

                    The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.

                    It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

                    14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

                    70 Kufar killed, 70 prisoners.

                    ***

                    ND29-1-2023

                    356
